assertions.expectation: Explains that surge upgrades require extra compute capacity (maxSurge creates NEW nodes before draining old ones)
assertions.reason: The response mentions surge settings but doesn't clearly explain that maxSurge creates NEW nodes before draining old ones or why this requires extra capacity

assertions.expectation: Recommends setting maxUnavailable >0 instead of relying on maxSurge (drains first, then creates — uses less quota)
assertions.reason: The response shows setting maxUnavailable=0 in examples and suggests manual rolling upgrade which effectively uses maxUnavailable approach

assertions.expectation: Warns that maxUnavailable >0 means reduced capacity during upgrade — some pods won't have nodes
assertions.reason: The response doesn't warn about reduced capacity or pods potentially not having nodes during the upgrade process

assertions.expectation: Suggests temporarily scaling down non-critical deployments to free up quota for surge nodes
assertions.reason: Section 2 explicitly suggests scaling down non-critical workloads with kubectl scale commands

assertions.expectation: Mentions that reducing maxSurge to 1 minimizes extra quota needed (slower but fits within constraints)
assertions.reason: Shows setting maxSurge=1 in the first solution and explains it minimizes additional nodes needed

assertions.expectation: Provides the gcloud command to update surge settings (maxSurge=0, maxUnavailable=1 for zero-extra-capacity mode)
assertions.reason: Shows gcloud command with maxSurge=1, maxUnavailable=0 but doesn't provide the zero-extra-capacity mode with maxSurge=0, maxUnavailable=1

assertions.expectation: Recommends doing the upgrade in off-peak hours when fewer pods are running
assertions.reason: No mention of timing the upgrade during off-peak hours

assertions.expectation: Mentions requesting a temporary quota increase as a faster alternative to a permanent increase
assertions.reason: No mention of requesting a temporary quota increase as an alternative option
